text: Hello everybody! I have some big news to share with you.
I'm actually going back to the US soon for a long trip.
I'll be there for three months, so I was thinking about things I haven't done yet in Korea
that I really want to do before I leave.
So today I am doing some things on my Korean bucket list.
I am very excited. It is 11 a.m. now and I'm quite hungry for breakfast, so I'm going to go
to a restaurant and try a Korean food that I have never had before.
Any guesses what it is? It is Salangtang, 9 years living in Korea and I've never tried
Salangtang before. Let's head to the restaurant.
I got my food and it's actually quite busy here. I think it's a matcheep. It seems very popular.
You can see the steam from the soup. I always like to start with the side dishes.
I think the dressing is so good because I can really taste the sesame oil and I love the
flavor of sesame. These pieces of radish are so big. Oh my gosh. I'm excited for the crunch.
So good. Super tangy and fresh. First bite ever of this dish. I'll try the broth first.
The flavor is actually quite subtle. It's not a very strong flavor, but it's good. I've heard
that this soup is very healthy because of the bone broth. It has a lot of collagen, so I hope
it's good for my skin. First bite of the meat. Wow, the meat is very soft and tender.
I think the kimchi will complement this very well since the kimchi is quite spicy and
has a bold flavor. It will go perfect with this soup.
That place was popping. I think I chose a good restaurant to try that for the first time.
I will definitely order it again in the future. So now, after eating a meal, I'm very full.
I need to walk a little bit, so I am heading to the meal flea market. I love antiques and
vintage shopping. I've never been there before, so I'm going to hop on a bus to go to Dongmyo.
I have made it to Dongmyo flea market. The first thing I did was pick up some coffee because I am a
little bit sleepy after such a big meal for breakfast. I don't really have anything in particular
that I want to buy at the markets today, so I'm just going to kind of browse. But if I happen to
find some nice sunglasses, maybe I'll buy a pair because recently my sunglasses broke or since
winter is coming, maybe a nice sweater or hoodie, something like that. So I'll keep my eyes out
for those items. Otherwise, I'm just going to enjoy the environment of the markets.
I decided to come back home for a little while. I actually killed two birds with one stone because
I shopped at the market, which was on my bucket list, and I also bought some yogurts from the yogur
lady. This has been something I've wanted to do in Korea for a very long time, but for some reason,
whenever I see the yogurts ladies driving their carts on the street, I get really nervous and shy,
but today I faced my fears. So I got this one, which is classic, and I do know this one,
but I've never tried it. And I also asked for another recommendation, and she gave me this
wheel, so something full of probiotics. That's good for digestion. So I am going to try both
of these in a minute, but first, let me review the market a little bit. So usually I like having
a partner in crime when I go to places like that because I am so indecisive, but I did end up
buying some sunglasses. I'll try them on. What do we think guys? As I walked around Dongmyo market,
it actually reminded me of something similar we have back home. So in the US, we do have
flea markets here and there, but where I'm from in the summertime, we also do something called
garage sales. It's very common for neighborhoods to have a garage sale weekend, where every house
in the neighborhood will open up their garages and set things outside for people to come walk around
and shop and buy some secondhand goods. I feel like in markets like Dongmyo, you really have to dig
to find something good. So we have a saying, one man's trash is another man's treasure,
and before I went to this market, I heard some people say, oh, it's just full of junk. But I heard
other people say that it's a gem and you must visit Dongmyo. So I had no clue what to expect,
but I did enjoy it. I like that atmosphere a lot. I feel like I'm stepping back in time.
So I don't know, the vibe is just very special there. Anyways, this morning has been a big success
so far, and now let's see if I love the drinks from the yogurt lady.
Is this healthy? It tastes very sweet to me. It tastes like candy in liquid form.
Wow, this is really good. Okay, let's try this one.
Dang, this is so delicious. Oh my gosh, I love both of these. I'm kind of sad that I did not
do this sooner. What a gem the yogurt ladies are. Oh, I love it. So anyways, the first half of my bucket
list day is done. And now I am heading out to meet up with my friend Doho. We have a few more fun

from the fortune teller tonight. I just finished my Saju and it was a very interesting reading.
Definitely not what I expected at all. She basically said that I'm going to have a lot of young

I don't like to play shooter games or anything to intense. But yeah, this is something I've always
wanted to do. So it'll be a new experience. I'm excited too. Well, for the Roman probably, right?

I feel like there's a huge learning curve at the PC Bang. I've never felt so incompetent in my
entire life. And getting everything set up at the PC Bang as someone who is not a gamer was

I watched Netflix while I was waiting for my game to download and that was about it. My friend also
watched Netflix because we had no clue what we were doing. But it looked really fun when I saw
other people gaming. So I really want to try again. If you have any game recommendations,
please let me know. Even though that part of the day was kind of a fail, my friend and I were laughing
so hard about it and it was a great memory. The whole day was a lot of fun and I'm glad I could
finally check some things off of my Korean bucket list. I hope you enjoyed spending the day with me.
I will be making a lot more content like this. So please make sure to subscribe to my channel
